title: Neoregelia coriacea
intro: Neoregelia coriacea, also known as the Brazilian Vase Plant, is a species of bromeliad native to Brazil, characterized by its striking, vase-shaped inflorescence. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds and infections. Bioactive constituents of Neoregelia coriacea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat various ailments and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Neoregelia coriacea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This family is commonly known as the pineapple family and includes over 3,000 species of plants. Neoregelia coriacea is native to the tropical regions of South America and is often grown as an ornamental plant for its unique and vibrant foliage. The plant's classification also places it within the genus Neoregelia, which is a group of epiphytic plants that are commonly found in the tropical rainforests of South America. Neoregelia coriacea is a popular choice among bromeliad enthusiasts due to its striking appearance and relatively low maintenance requirements.
